description: Increased risk of bleeding. Warfarin inhibits clotting factors while aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation, creating additive hemorrhagic risk.
management: Avoid if possible. If combination necessary, monitor INR closely, consider gastroprotection with PPI, educate on bleeding signs.

description: NSAIDs increase bleeding risk with warfarin via platelet inhibition and GI mucosal damage. INR may be elevated.
management: Avoid NSAIDs with warfarin. Use acetaminophen for pain. If NSAID required, use lowest dose for shortest duration with PPI.

description: Risk of lactic acidosis. Contrast dye may impair renal function, reducing metformin clearance and causing accumulation.
management: Withhold metformin 48h before and after contrast procedures. Check renal function before restarting.

description: Serotonin syndrome risk. SSRIs combined with MAOIs cause dangerous serotonin accumulation. Potentially fatal.
management: NEVER combine. Allow 5 weeks washout after fluoxetine before starting MAOI (14 days for other SSRIs).

description: CYP3A4 inhibition by erythromycin increases statin levels 6-10x, causing rhabdomyolysis risk.
management: Do not use together. Switch to pravastatin or rosuvastatin (not CYP3A4 metabolized) if macrolide needed.

description: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate renal clearance, increasing toxicity. Risk of pancytopenia and mucositis.
management: Avoid NSAIDs with high-dose methotrexate. For low-dose (RA), monitor closely and use with caution.

description: Amiodarone increases digoxin levels 70-100% by reducing renal and non-renal clearance. Risk of digoxin toxicity.
management: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor serum digoxin levels closely.

description: NSAIDs reduce lithium renal clearance, increasing serum levels 15-50%. Risk of lithium toxicity.
management: Monitor lithium levels when adding or changing NSAIDs. Consider sulindac (least effect) if NSAID needed.

description: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone, decreasing potassium excretion. Supplemental potassium causes dangerous hyperkalemia.
management: Avoid potassium supplements with ACE inhibitors unless documented hypokalemia. Monitor serum potassium.

description: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, blocking conversion of clopidogrel to active metabolite. Reduces antiplatelet effect 40-50%.
management: Use pantoprazole instead (minimal CYP2C19 effect). Avoid omeprazole and esomeprazole with clopidogrel.

description: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, increasing theophylline levels. Narrow therapeutic index makes toxicity likely.
management: Monitor theophylline levels. Reduce dose by 30-50% if ciprofloxacin required. Consider levofloxacin (less CYP1A2 effect).

description: Both drugs increase serotonin. Combination increases serotonin syndrome risk and lowers seizure threshold.
management: Use lowest effective doses. Monitor for serotonin syndrome. Consider alternative analgesic.

description: Strong CYP3A4 inhibition increases statin levels, causing rhabdomyolysis.
management: Contraindicated. Suspend statin during clarithromycin course or switch to azithromycin.

description: Both drugs cause potassium retention. Combined hyperkalemia risk is significant, especially with renal impairment.
management: Monitor potassium within 1 week of starting combination, then monthly. Use low doses. Avoid if eGFR < 30.

description: Rifampin is a potent CYP inducer, increasing warfarin metabolism 3-5x. INR drops dramatically within days.
management: Avoid combination. If unavoidable, increase warfarin dose 2-3x and monitor INR every 2-3 days. Adjust when rifampin stops.

description: Disulfiram-like reaction. Metronidazole inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase, causing acetaldehyde accumulation.
management: Avoid alcohol during treatment and for 48h after completing metronidazole.

description: Both cause vasodilation via nitric oxide pathway. Combined use causes severe, potentially fatal hypotension.
management: Absolutely contraindicated. Allow 24h (48h for tadalafil) after PDE5 inhibitor before nitrate use.

description: Fluconazole inhibits CYP2C9, the primary warfarin metabolizing enzyme. INR may increase 2-3x.
management: Reduce warfarin dose by 25-50% when starting fluconazole. Monitor INR within 3-5 days.

description: Complex bidirectional interaction. Carbamazepine induces valproate metabolism while valproate inhibits carbamazepine epoxide clearance.
management: Monitor both drug levels. Adjust doses based on clinical response and toxicity signs.

description: Trimethoprim acts as potassium-sparing diuretic (blocks ENaC). Combined with ACE inhibitor, severe hyperkalemia risk.
management: Monitor potassium within 2 days of starting TMP. Use alternative antibiotic if possible. Increased risk in elderly and CKD.

description: Both cause CNS and respiratory depression. Combined use is the leading cause of prescription drug overdose deaths.
management: Avoid concurrent prescribing when possible. If combination necessary, use lowest effective doses, limit duration. FDA Black Box Warning.

description: Valproate displaces phenytoin from protein binding and inhibits its metabolism, increasing free phenytoin levels despite normal total levels.
management: Monitor free (unbound) phenytoin levels, not total. Adjust valproate based on clinical response.

description: Spironolactone retains potassium by blocking aldosterone. Additional potassium supplementation causes dangerous hyperkalemia.
management: Do not supplement potassium with spironolactone unless documented severe hypokalemia. Monitor potassium closely.

description: Grapefruit contains furanocoumarins that irreversibly inhibit intestinal CYP3A4, increasing simvastatin bioavailability up to 15x.
management: Avoid grapefruit with simvastatin and lovastatin. Atorvastatin: limit to small quantities. Pravastatin/rosuvastatin: no interaction.

description: SSRIs impair platelet serotonin uptake, and NSAIDs inhibit COX. Combined GI bleeding risk increased 3-15x.
management: Add gastroprotection (PPI) if combination necessary. Monitor for GI symptoms.

description: Amiodarone inhibits CYP2C9 and CYP3A4, increasing warfarin effect. INR rises 30-50% within 1-2 weeks and persists months after stopping.
management: Reduce warfarin by 30-50% when starting amiodarone. Monitor INR weekly for 4-6 weeks. Effect persists 1-3 months after amiodarone discontinuation.

description: Divalent/trivalent cations (Al, Mg, Ca, Fe) chelate fluoroquinolones, reducing absorption by 90%.
management: Take ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids, calcium, iron, or dairy products.

description: Calcium chelates levothyroxine in the gut, reducing absorption by 20-25%. May cause hypothyroidism.
management: Separate by at least 4 hours. Take levothyroxine on empty stomach, 30-60 minutes before food/supplements.

description: Calcium chelates tetracyclines, reducing absorption by up to 80%. Treatment failure likely.
management: Take tetracycline 1 hour before or 2 hours after dairy, calcium, iron, or antacids.

description: Alcohol potentiates metformin's lactate-producing effects and impairs hepatic gluconeogenesis, increasing lactic acidosis risk.
management: Limit alcohol consumption. Avoid binge drinking. Higher risk with liver disease or fasting.
